rulings.Case Title: HERMANA GRANT VS. MICHAEL FREEMAN-MCGUIRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Representative Of Estate For Deceased Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 6, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF HERMANA GRANT Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Representative Of Estate For Deceased Deft Granted. Plaintiff has complied with the procedures for substitution for the deceased Defendant. (CCP § 377.41; Prob. Code § 9370(a).) On March 12, 2012, Defendant died. (Motion, Wolford Decl., 3.) On July 23, 2012, Plaintiff filed a claim against Defendant's estate. (Id. 4). On September 20, 2012, Defendant's personal representative rejected the claim. (Id. 5.) On November 20, 2012, Plaintiff filed the instant motion, which is within 3 months after rejection of the claim. In opposition, Defendant attempts to adjudicate the merits of this action, which is improper for this motion. Similarly, Defendant's request to renew the request to remove Ms. Shelton as Plaintiff's personal representative in opposition to this motion is improper. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LUPE SAVALA PALOMARES VS. NOR-CAL SCAFFOLDING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motioj To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 6, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF LUPE PALOMARES' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions Set One. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and Denied in part. Defendant is ordered to provide amended responses which comply with CCP secs 2033.220(b), (c) and 2033.230. The Court overrules all "legal conclusion" and "assumes facts" objections. See Burke v. Sup. Ct. (1969) 71 Cal. 2d 276; CCP sec. 2033.010. The Court denies the motion as to the other objections, however Defendant is still required to comply with secs. 2033.220(b) and (c), specifically providing responses that "reasonably and clearly" qualify that which is admitted or denied based on any remaining objections. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JASON COOK VS. MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 6, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Summary judgment is granted. Defendant Mercedes has established that it cannot be liable for express or implied warranties because it was not the manufacturer of the car. Mercedes shifts the burden on this issue based on the Dingman Declaration. In opposition, Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff does not dispute that Mercedes was not the manufacturer. (UMF 1). Plaintiff's Amended Complaint 7 only alleges that Mercedes was the manufacturer - not the distributor-and Plaintiff cannot avoid summary judgment by raising unpleaded theories of liability. Further, the Dingman deposition establishes that Mercedes is not the distributor. At most, Plaintiff has shown that the Mercedes website is not entirely accurate. In any event, Exhibit J to the Johnson Declaration (the page from the Mercedes website) is from January 17, 2012, which is well after the relevant dates in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JASON COOK VS. MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 6, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT DAIMLER VEHICLE INNOVATIONS USA LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion is denied in its entirety. Defendant Daimler fails to shift the burden on issue 1 (cause of action per CC 1793.2(d)(2) fails) because it failed to provide a copy of the written warranty with its moving papers. The warranty was not attached to the Gold declaration, although referred to. The Court has not had the opportunity to review the evidence. Defendant fails to shift the burden on issue 2 (implied warranty) based on Mexia v. Rinker Boat Co. Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1297. Defendant fails to shift the burden on issues 3 and 4 (remedies) because defendant has not shown that the claims cannot be established. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROSARIO M. O'CONNOR et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of M9otion Andm Motion To Compel Further Production And Further Responses To Pltf'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 6, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS' Motion To Compel Further Production And Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is granted in part and denied in part. RFPD 14 - Denied. The Request as written is overbroad. RFPD 20-23 - Granted. Defendant is to produce responsive documents relating to conduct similar to that alleged in paragraphs 72, 73 and 75 of the First Amended Complaint. RFPD 24-27 - Granted. Defendant is to produce responsive documents relating to conduct similar to that alleged in paragraphs 72, 73 and 75 of the First Amended Complaint. RFPD 28-30 - Granted. Defendant is produce responsive documents relating to the policies and procedures for conduct alleged in paragraphs 72, 73 and 75 of the First Amended Complaint. RFPD 32-36 - Per the parties agreement, the Court orders that amended responses as to 32, 33, 35 and 36 will be produced and that responsive documents will be produced as to 34. RFPD 47-58 - Per the parties agreement, the Court orders that amended responses as to 47-52 will be produced and responsive documents will be produced as to 53 and 54. With regard to 55-58, the Court orders that Wells Fargo Bank notify the Plaintiff of the third parties' response or lack thereof 21 days after the notification was provided to the third parties. If there is no objection by the third parties, the responsive documents will be produced 20 days after notification to the Plaintiff. If the third parties object to production, Plaintiff has the right to file appropriate motions to compel production. The court orders that all amended responses and documents ordered to be produced shall be provided no later than 25 days after entry of this order, unless otherwise specifically delineated. The Court expects the parties to comply with their other agreements including redaction of private identifying information and entering into a protective order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE KELLY VS. GEORGE POON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 6, 2013, Line 18, PLAINTIFF GEORGE KELLY MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT Granted. No opposition filed. The request for attorneys fees in the prayer for relief is stricken. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SAFEWAY INC. VS. MCKESSON CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 6, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF SAFEWAY INC. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE Continued on the court's own motion to February 25, 2013 to give Safeway, Inc. the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Safeway, Inc. must immediately deliver courtesy copies of its moving papers to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, or the motion will be denied. = (302/MJM)
